aquarium: [{'aquarium': 'A physical, three-dimensional, transparent container, typically made of glass or acrylic, filled with water and designed to house and support actual aquatic life such as fish, invertebrates, or plants. It is characterized by a visible waterline, a substrate of gravel or sand at the bottom, and often includes decorative elements like artificial plants, rocks, or ornaments. The container is illuminated and equipped with functional systems such as filters, heaters, or pumps to maintain a stable aquatic environment. This object is distinguished by its physical, three-dimensional structure, the presence of water, living aquatic organisms, and a functioning aquatic ecosystem. It excludes non-living, non-functional depictions such as two-dimensional images, printed representations, or visual simulations; decorative figurines or sculptures that mimic an aquarium but do not support aquatic life; and any non-aquatic containers such as kitchen cookware or household appliances (e.g., washing machines), even if they are transparent or contain water, as they lack the biological and ecological context of a true aquatic habitat.'}]
atomizer: [{'atomizer': 'A self-contained, portable container designed specifically for atomization, featuring a functional spray mechanism (such as a mechanical pump or pressurized valve) that actively produces a fine mist. The object must have a body capable of holding liquid, typically cylindrical or rectangular, made of transparent or opaque plastic or metal, with a nozzle or actuator at the top for operation. It must visibly demonstrate a spray function through active atomization. The object must be a functional container designed for atomization, not for storage, display, or other purposes, and must not be a non-functional decorative element, a structural component of a building, or a non-atomizing object that merely resembles an atomizer in appearance. It must be a standalone unit, not a fixed, non-liquid-containing structure, and must not be a utility box, cabinet, or other non-functional architectural element, even if it is mounted or resembles a container. The object must not be a person, a digital device, or any non-liquid-containing entity, and must not be a flat, non-liquid-containing surface or a floor covering. Additionally, the object must not be a container used for food storage, cooking, or other non-atomizing purposes, even if it has a similar shape or design to an atomizer. The object must not be a tall, vertical, fixed structure such as a utility pole or tower, even if it has a cylindrical shape, and must not be a structural component of a landscape or infrastructure. The object must not be a utensil, dish, or decorative item, even if it is small, shallow, or has a container-like appearance, and must not be designed for holding or serving food or condiments. The object must not be a cleaning tool, mop, or similar implement, even if it has a cylindrical shape or is used in a household setting, and must not be a non-atomizing object that merely resembles an atomizer in appearance. Furthermore, the object must not be a fixed plumbing fixture (such as a faucet or showerhead) that delivers liquid through a spray or stream, even if it has a spray function, as it is not a container designed for atomization. The object must be a functional container designed for atomization, not a non-functional decorative element, even if it has a similar shape or design. The object must be small and portable, not a large, fixed, or non-liquid-containing structure, and must not be a non-atomizing container used for storage or other non-atomizing purposes. The object must not be a kitchen appliance, utility device, or any non-atomizing device, even if it is portable and has a similar shape to an atomizer, and must not be a toaster, blender, or similar appliance, even if it has a handle or control panel. The object must be designed to dispense liquid in a fine mist, not for other purposes such as storage, display, or structural support.'}]
barrette: [{'barrette': 'A small, rigid, elongated accessory made of a smooth, polished material such as plastic or metal, designed to be worn directly on the head to hold hair in place or as a decorative element. It features a narrow, continuous band that wraps around a section of hair, typically positioned at the back or side of the head. The object is not mounted on a surface, not used as a grooming tool, and not intended for covering the body. It is exclusively used for personal adornment or hair management and is physically worn on the head as a functional hair accessory. It is not a soft, draped textile, not a garment, and not a decorative display. It is not an edible item, not a tool for styling hair, and not used for culinary or personal care purposes. It is not a living creature, not a natural object, and not part of a mechanical or structural system. It is specifically designed for head-worn use and is not a non-wearable, illustrative, or representational object that merely resembles a hair fastener. It is not a fixed, non-portable structure, not a sign, not a display, and not a decorative element that is not worn. It is not a handheld electronic device, not a tool for communication, and not used for recording or playback purposes. The object is small in scale, portable, and worn directly on the head, distinguishing it from large, stationary, or non-portable structures that may share a similar shape or color. It is not a cleaning tool, not a household implement, and not used for floor maintenance or sanitation. The object must be worn on the head as a functional hair fastener or decorative element, and not be part of a costume, display, or performance that uses a similar shape or color for non-hair-related purposes. It is not a component of a vehicle or device, not used for signaling or illumination, and not a light-emitting object, even if it shares a similar shape or color. The object is not mounted on a surface or part of a larger structure, and is not used for storage or transport. It is not a full head covering, not a hat, not a cap, and not a garment worn on the head, but a small accessory that is not intended to cover the head or face.'}]
bathrobe: [{'bathrobe': 'A full-length, loose-fitting, absorbent garment made of soft textile material such as terry cloth, worn on the body after bathing or showering. It features long sleeves, a collar, and a front closure secured with a belt or sash, designed for comfort and moisture absorption. The garment is worn in private, domestic environments such as bathrooms or bedrooms, and is a standalone, wearable textile item, not a component of a vehicle, building, or other mechanical structure, and not a decorative accessory, functional tool, or non-wearable absorbent item (such as a kitchen towel or cloth). It is specifically designed for direct body contact and personal comfort, and is distinct from small fabric patches, emblems, or non-wearable items, even if they share absorbent materials or are used in similar settings. The bathrobe is a complete, wearable garment intended to be worn over the body, not a small fabric item placed on surfaces or used for other purposes, nor a costume accessory, performance prop, or decorative item, even if it is worn in a personal or domestic context. The garment must be made of soft, absorbent material suitable for drying the body and providing comfort, and not of non-absorbent, synthetic, or novelty materials such as metallic or plastic-like fabrics.'}]
bib: [{'bib': 'A bib is a piece of soft, absorbent fabric worn around the neck and over the front of a person’s torso, specifically by infants or young children during feeding, to protect clothing from food spills. It is secured around the neck with ties, snaps, or a clip, and is designed to be easily removable and washable. The bib is physically attached to a human wearer and is used exclusively in a feeding context to catch spills, distinguishing it from inanimate objects, food items, temporary skin applications, decorative neckwear, or protective coverings used in non-feeding contexts such as rain protection. It is worn by humans, not animals or toys, and must be a wearable garment, not a handheld device, object placed on a surface, or a fixed structural element of a building. It is not a decorative item, plush toy, or display object, and its primary function is to absorb and contain food during eating. The bib is worn in a way that it covers the chest and upper torso, and is positioned to intercept food that may fall during consumption. It is not a mechanical or structural object, even if it shares a superficial shape or placement, and must be made of fabric or a similar soft material, not metal, rubber, or other rigid materials. The bib is exclusively used in a feeding context and is not worn for decoration, warmth, or protection from non-food-related sources. It is not a floor mat, rug, or any other stationary object placed on a surface, and must be worn by a human to be considered a bib. Objects that are made of fabric and placed on surfaces, even if they resemble a bib in appearance, are not considered bibs unless they are worn by a human in a feeding context. Additionally, objects worn by animals or attached to animal gear, even if they resemble a bib in shape or placement, are not considered bibs. Reflections, transparent surfaces, or fabric items that are not physically worn by a human in a feeding context are also excluded. Furthermore, any food item, regardless of its shape or placement, is not a bib, even if it visually resembles a bib or is placed on a surface. The bib must be worn by a human in a feeding context and cannot be a stationary or attached object, such as a container or equipment mounted on a vehicle. Decorative bib-shaped items worn by toys, dolls, or in non-feeding contexts are explicitly excluded, as they do not serve the functional purpose of catching food spills during consumption.'}]
business_card: [{'business_card': 'Small, rectangular, flat items made of paper or thin plastic, designed to be held in the hand or displayed in a holder. They have a smooth, often glossy surface and are used as personal communication tools, specifically to convey contact information such as name, title, and organization. These items are commonly found in environments where personal organization or professional networking is a focus, and are typically not framed or mounted on walls. They are often stored in a stand or holder for easy access and visibility. The primary distinguishing feature is that they are specifically designed to convey personal or professional contact details and are not used for other functional or decorative purposes. They are intended for personal, portable use in social or professional interactions and are not worn on the body, nor are they designed for display as part of a costume or performance, and are not used for theatrical or recreational purposes. They are static, non-interactive objects that serve as a personal identifier and are not living organisms. They are not consumable, not electronic, not a piece of clothing or costume accessory, and are not used for culinary, entertainment, or decorative purposes. They are not mounted on stands or used as canvases, whiteboards, or display surfaces for writing or artistic purposes. They are not placed on tables as part of a meal setting and are not used in conjunction with food items. They are not framed or mounted on walls and are not used for decorative purposes. They are not functional tools, such as kitchen utensils, and are not used in food preparation or culinary contexts. They are not affixed to surfaces or used in non-professional contexts, and are not used for decorative or informational purposes unrelated to personal or professional contact details. They are not large, functional objects such as vehicles or machinery, and are not designed for transportation, utility, or industrial purposes. They are not used in contexts where their primary function is not to convey personal or professional contact information. They are inanimate objects, not living organisms, and are not used for entertainment or theatrical purposes. They are not affixed to public structures or used in public utility contexts, and are not designed for public display or functional utility beyond personal identification. They are not used for general note-taking, labeling, or temporary information storage, and are not intended for use as personal reminders or labels. They are not electronic devices, nor are they used for data transmission, networking, or other technological functions. They are not containers, pouches, or storage items, and are not used for carrying personal belongings or equipment. They are not part of a model or display setup, and are not used in contexts where they are integrated into a larger decorative or functional environment. They are not used in dining settings or as part of meal arrangements, and are not placed on tables in conjunction with food items. They are not part of a larger, non-functional, artistic or decorative creation, and are not embedded within a sculptural or thematic context. They are standalone objects designed for personal identification and professional networking, and are not used as decorative elements in cakes, sculptures, or other non-functional artistic displays. They are not worn on the body, not used in ceremonial or festive contexts, and are not part of a costume or performance.'}]
camcorder: [{'camcorder': 'A portable, self-contained electronic device designed for capturing video and still images, featuring a main body with a lens assembly that includes a prominent front element and a barrel with focus or zoom controls, a flash, and a viewfinder or display screen. It contains internal electronic components such as a sensor, processor, and power source, enabling it to record and store media independently. The device is ergonomically shaped for handheld use or tripod mounting, with a form factor optimized for personal or professional videography. It is functionally distinct from non-imaging devices (e.g., remote controls, kitchen appliances, grooming tools), non-electronic objects (e.g., toys, fabric items, living animals), and structural or architectural features, as it must possess a lens and flash to perform imaging. It is also differentiated from stationary imaging devices (e.g., webcams, security cameras) by its portable design, dedicated recording capabilities, and intended use in mobile video capture. It must not be a peripheral device (e.g., webcam), general-purpose computing device (e.g., smartphone, tablet), or non-functional replica, as it must be a fully operational imaging device with a primary function of video and image recording.'}]
cape: [{'cape': "A lightweight, sleeveless, open-fronted fabric garment worn over the shoulders and back, typically extending down the torso and often reaching the knees or lower. It is made from a draping, non-rigid material that exhibits natural drape and flexibility, and is designed as a wearable, temporary outer layer, commonly used as a protective covering during activities such as haircuts, or as a ceremonial or thematic accessory. The garment must be physically wearable by a human and functionally used as a protective or decorative outer layer, not as a permanent architectural feature, a rigid electronic device, or a non-fabric item such as a figurine accessory, a food item, or a decorative element on furniture. The object must be a human-worn item, not a living animal or a non-fabric object that visually resembles a cape, such as a kitten in a shoe, even if it is placed in a similar context. The garment must be a physical, wearable fabric item, not a visual imitation or sticker on another object, and must not be a mechanical, structural, or functional component of a vehicle, machine, or other non-fabric system, nor a permanent fixture or consumable object. Additionally, the object must not be a living animal or a non-fabric item that visually resembles a cape, such as a kitten in a shoe, even if it is placed in a similar context. The garment must be a fabric-based garment, not a non-fabric object such as a toy, figurine, or decorative item, even if it is shaped or colored to resemble a cape. The garment must be capable of being worn by a human and must not be a static, non-draping, non-flexible attachment to a non-human object or a decorative element on a collectible figure. Furthermore, the object must not be a fabric item used as a temporary covering, such as a napkin, cloth, or towel, even if it is draped or shaped like a cape, nor a non-fabric item used as a covering, such as a plastic or metal object, even if it is placed in a similar context. The garment must not be a structured, form-fitting, sewn piece of clothing that is part of a complete outfit or ceremonial attire, nor a permanent or integral component of a clothing ensemble, even if it has a cape-like appearance. The garment must not be a component of a non-human entity's equipment, such as a horse's saddle or a vehicle's attachment, even if it is made of fabric and has a cape-like shape. The garment must be a true fabric outer layer, not a disposable or non-draping covering used in a functional context, and must exhibit natural drape and flexibility, not be rigid or non-fabric. The garment must not be a functional tool, household item, or cleaning implement, even if it has a similar shape or is placed in a context that might suggest a cape-like appearance, and must not be a non-fabric object used for cleaning, decoration, or utility, even if it is shaped or positioned in a way that resembles a cape. The garment must not be a visual representation, reflection, shadow, or printed image on a surface, even if it appears to have the shape or context of a cape, and must be a tangible, physical fabric item that can be worn by a human. The garment must not be a flat, two-dimensional graphic or pattern on a non-fabric surface, even if it is shaped like a cape, nor a digital or projected image, and must be a three-dimensional, wearable fabric item with natural drape and flexibility. The garment must not be a rigid, non-draping object held or used by a person, even if it is in a context that might suggest a cape-like appearance, such as a smartphone or a tablet, and must not be a non-fabric object used for communication, entertainment, or utility, even if it is positioned in a way that resembles a cape."}]
casserole: [{'casserole': "A baked food item, typically served as a main course or side dish, characterized by a golden-brown, crispy, and textured surface formed by roasting or baking. It is contained within a deep, dish-like vessel—rectangular, oval, round, or other shape—made of ceramic or glass, with the container visible around the edges and integral to its preparation and presentation. The food is a solid, cohesive, edible mass, indicating it has been cooked and set in a single serving vessel. It is clearly identifiable as a food product, not a non-edible object, and must not be confused with non-baked food items, raw mixtures, liquids, or non-edible objects such as structural elements, decorative patterns, or tools, even if they are placed in a dish or have a similar shape. The container is an essential part of the food's preparation and presentation, not a separate serving vessel or decorative item. The object must be a cooked food item, not a natural or decorative item that merely resembles food in appearance. The food must be contained in a deep dish, distinguishing it from flat, open-faced baked items such as pizzas or flatbreads, and from flat, uncooked food items like tortillas. The object must be a solid, cohesive, edible mass, not a flat, non-edible item with a textured surface, even if it is held in a dish-like container. The item must be clearly identifiable as a food product, not a non-edible object, and must not be mistaken for a decorative or functional object that happens to be placed in a dish or has a similar appearance. The object must be a food item, not a cooking vessel, and must not be confused with pots, pans, or other non-edible cooking tools, even if they are placed on a stovetop or have a similar shape. The object must be a food product, not a non-edible tool or cleaning implement, even if it is placed in a dish or has a similar appearance. The object must not be a person or animal, even if they are placed in a dish-like container or appear to be part of a food presentation. The object must be clearly identifiable as a food product, not a non-edible object, and must not be mistaken for a structural or decorative element, even if it is placed in a dish-like container or has a similar appearance. The object must be in a context consistent with food preparation or serving, such as a kitchen, dining table, or oven, and must not be part of a non-food-related setting such as a street, building facade, or outdoor environment. The object must not be a flat, open-faced baked item with a thin crust and toppings, even if it is served in a dish, as this is not a casserole. The food must be a solid, cohesive, edible mass, not a flat, open-faced item with a thin crust and toppings, even if it is served in a dish, as this is not a casserole."}]
coleslaw: A side dish consisting primarily of finely shredded raw cabbage with a salad dressing.
costume: [{'costume': 'A wearable, full-body suit designed to completely transform the wearer’s appearance by mimicking the form, color, and features of a specific non-human entity, such as an animal, fictional character, or whimsical figure. The suit is form-fitting, covers the entire body including the head, arms, and legs, and is constructed from fabric or similar material to create a realistic illusion. It is worn by a person for the purpose of impersonation, entertainment, or performance, and is distinguishable from living beings by its lack of biological movement, its ability to be worn, and its transformative nature. The costume must fully obscure the wearer’s natural form and not be a partial accessory, functional garment, or item of clothing used in everyday contexts. It must be a complete, integrated garment worn by a person, and not a non-wearable structure, architectural feature, inanimate object, food item, or decorative display such as a figurine, ornament, statue, or food arrangement. Partial garments such as capes, cloaks, or accessories that do not fully cover the body or transform the wearer’s appearance are excluded from this definition. The costume must be a man-made, non-living object and cannot be a living animal or biological entity, even if it visually resembles one. Additionally, the object must be designed specifically for wear and transformation, and not be a functional tool, everyday object, or item used for non-impersonation purposes. The costume must be a wearable garment, not a non-wearable object such as a kite, inflatable, or decorative figure shaped like a character, even if it is three-dimensional and visually resembles a costume, as it is not designed to be worn by a person and does not transform the wearer’s appearance. The costume must not consist of a small printed graphic, logo, or design on a piece of clothing or any surface, including floors, walls, or play mats; it must be a three-dimensional, wearable garment that fully covers the body and is intended for impersonation or performance. The costume must not include ceremonial or symbolic garments (e.g., academic regalia, religious vestments, or professional uniforms) that are worn for identification or tradition rather than transformation into a non-human entity. Furthermore, the costume must not be a functional accessory or part of a vehicle, even if it is shaped like a character or object, and must not serve a practical purpose such as storage or utility. The costume must be a complete, three-dimensional, wearable garment designed specifically for impersonation or performance, and not a functional tool, everyday object, or decorative item, even if it is shaped like a character. It must not be a small, non-transformative accessory such as a tiara, crown, or headpiece, even if worn on the body, as it does not fully cover the body or transform the wearer’s appearance into a non-human entity. Additionally, the costume must not be a non-wearable object such as a kite, inflatable, or decorative figure shaped like a character, even if it is three-dimensional and visually resembles a costume, as it is not designed to be worn by a person and does not transform the wearer’s appearance.'}]
cream_pitcher: [{'cream_pitcher': 'A small, cylindrical or slightly bulbous container with a handle and a spout, typically made of ceramic or glass, designed for holding and pouring liquid cream or similar condiments. It features a smooth, often glossy surface and is constructed to allow controlled pouring. The object is functionally distinct as a piece of tableware, commonly used in dining settings and placed near beverages such as coffee or tea. It is distinguished from decorative items, non-pourable containers, and larger kitchen appliances by its specific form, size, and intended use for serving condiments.'}]
cub_(animal): [{'cub_(animal)': 'A young, non-human mammal of the bear species, characterized by its small size, thick dark fur, quadrupedal stance, and natural behaviors such as foraging or walking on the ground; it is typically found in natural outdoor environments, often in proximity to an adult of the same species, and is a biological animal, not a human in costume or disguise, nor a toy, statue, or artificial representation. The cub is distinguished by its juvenile proportions, including a relatively large head, short limbs, and a compact body, and is significantly smaller than an adult bear, which are significantly larger, have different fur coloration (often lighter or grizzled), and exhibit mature body structure and facial features. This class includes only real, living bear cubs and excludes any non-living or non-biological representations that do not accurately reflect the physical appearance, posture, and proportions of a real juvenile cub. The cub is typically observed in a terrestrial setting, with dry fur and a natural, alert posture, and is not to be confused with cubs in atypical contexts such as being fully submerged in water or engaged in highly dynamic, rearing behaviors that may suggest play or interaction with an adult, unless such behaviors are consistent with natural juvenile bear behavior in a natural environment. The object must exhibit signs of biological life, such as natural movement, breathing, and interaction with the environment, and must not be a static or inanimate replica.'}]
hair_curler: [{'hair_curler': 'A handheld electric device designed for hair styling, featuring an elongated body with two parallel, flat, metallic heating plates at one end, connected by a hinge mechanism that allows them to clamp down on hair. The device includes a non-slip casing, typically made of heat-resistant plastic, housing internal heating elements and control mechanisms. A power cord extends from the opposite end, indicating electrical operation. It is portable, compact, and intended for personal grooming use, distinct from non-electrical tools, decorative items, or kitchen appliances. The object must exhibit functional electrical and mechanical components, including visible heating plates, a hinge, and a power cord, and must not possess biological features or resemble non-functional, non-electrical objects in shape or context. It is not a storage item, container, or two-dimensional representation, and is not part of a larger structure or stationary fixture.'}]
diaper: [{'diaper': 'A soft, flexible, absorbent, single-piece undergarment designed to be worn around the lower torso and upper thighs of infants or young children. It features a waistband and leg cuffs to ensure a secure fit and is constructed from pliable, absorbent materials to manage bodily waste. The object is worn directly on the body for personal hygiene purposes and must not be rigid, non-absorbent, or non-wearable. It is not a food item, decorative object, toy, costume, prop, or structural element, regardless of shape, context, or placement. It must function as a wearable, absorbent hygiene product and not serve a decorative, ceremonial, or non-absorbent purpose.'}]
easel: [{'easel': 'A freestanding, portable support structure with a rigid frame and legs, featuring a large, flat, vertical surface designed to hold and display physical visual content such as paintings, diagrams, or presentations. It is elevated for use by a person and is distinct from wearable items, flexible materials, or fixed wall-mounted surfaces, and is not a flat, floor-based object, stationary seating structure, or handheld electronic device used for viewing content. It is also distinct from small, flat, surface-mounted electronic displays or monitors, regardless of their content, function, or mounting (including those attached to vehicles or as functional accessories). The display surface is typically non-electronic and designed for physical media such as paper, canvas, or board. It is not a cleaning tool, broom, mop, or any other functional implement with a vertical handle, regardless of its shape or size. It is not a fixed, non-portable structure used for a functional purpose such as communication, storage, or utility, even if it has a vertical surface or display screen. The object must be primarily intended for the display of visual content, not for functional utility, and must not be a household appliance or utility device with a vertical surface or door. It is commonly used in settings such as art demonstrations, performances, or public exhibitions where visual content is presented to an audience.'}]
eraser: [{'eraser': 'A small, non-mechanical object typically composed of a soft, pliable material, designed for the purpose of removing marks made by pencils. It is commonly shaped as a rectangular block, cylinder, or wedge, and is often associated with writing instruments such as pencils, either attached to them or found in proximity within writing or drawing contexts. The object is functionally distinct from marking tools, as it is not used to create marks, and is not intended for food, decoration, or household utility. It is inanimate, non-edible, and not used for storing or dispensing substances. Its structure and intended use differentiate it from other soft, non-reflective objects, even if they share similar physical properties.'}]
fig_(fruit): [{'fig_(fruit)': 'A fleshy, pulpy fruit with a soft, non-hollow interior containing numerous tiny seeds embedded in a fibrous, moist matrix. It has a typically ovoid or pear-shaped form with a smooth to slightly irregular surface, often exhibiting natural indentations or dimples. The fruit is botanically a syconium, formed from a cluster of flowers enclosed within a fleshy receptacle, resulting in a unique internal structure where seeds are embedded in the flesh. It is a natural biological product, organic in composition, with no mechanical, electronic, or synthetic components. The texture is soft and succulent, and the fruit lacks rigid, uniform surfaces or artificial features. It is not a manufactured imitation, toy, or decorative object, and does not resemble animal anatomy or digital devices.'}]
flute_glass: [{'flute_glass': 'A transparent, three-dimensional, functional drinking vessel made of glass, characterized by a tall, slender, and narrow bowl that tapers toward the rim, designed to preserve carbonation and enhance aroma in sparkling beverages. It features a long, thin stem that elevates the bowl to prevent heat transfer from the hand, and a flat, circular base that ensures stability. The object is a real, physical glass container capable of holding a carbonated beverage, with visible bubbles and foam, and exhibits physical depth, transparency, reflections, and shadows. It must not be a flat, printed, illustrated, or two-dimensional representation on any surface, nor a decorative or non-functional item. The object must be made of glass, not metal, plastic, or other opaque materials, and must possess a distinct bowl-stem-base structure with a reflective, transparent surface that allows visibility of the liquid inside. It must be a drinking vessel specifically designed for sparkling beverages, with a liquid content that includes visible carbonation and foam, and must be placed in a context consistent with beverage service, such as a dining or serving setting. The object must not be a costume accessory, headpiece, or any non-functional object that mimics the shape of a flute glass but serves no drinking purpose. It must not be a kitchen appliance, a decorative item, or any other functional object that is not a drinking vessel. The bowl must be tall and narrow with a tapering rim, and the liquid must contain visible carbonation and foam, distinguishing it from short, wide glasses or those containing non-carbonated liquids. The object must not be a short, wide glass without a stem, nor a glass designed for non-carbonated beverages.'}]
gargoyle: [{'gargoyle': "Three-dimensional sculptural figures, typically crafted from stone, ceramic, or metal, featuring a stylized, grotesque, or mythical appearance. These figures are permanently integrated into the exterior of a real building as architectural components, serving as decorative or symbolic elements, often resembling mythical creatures. They are solid, textured, and physically attached to the structure, distinguishing them from two-dimensional images, photographs, or representations such as printed depictions on vehicles, clothing, or electronic devices—regardless of context or use. Gargoyles are inanimate, non-living objects and must be distinguished from living animals or natural entities, even if they visually resemble mythical creatures. They are not interior decorations, functional objects, or items used in non-architectural contexts. Gargoyles are specifically designed and installed as part of a building's exterior structure, serving a functional or symbolic role in that context, and are physically integrated into the building’s facade. The object must be a permanent architectural feature, not a standalone decorative item, and must be a sculptural figure, not a functional object or a two-dimensional surface, even if it has artistic elements or graffiti. This includes excluding any object that is a printed or applied image—such as on a bag, clothing, or electronic device—regardless of its context or use, as well as modern electronic devices, such as routers or modems, even if they are placed in a room with architectural features. The object must be a fixed, non-functional architectural element, not a movable or utilitarian item, and must be a physical, three-dimensional sculpture, not a representation or depiction in a model, diorama, or digital display. The object must be a permanent architectural feature, not a temporary or decorative item, and must be affixed to the exterior of a building, not an interior surface or a standalone object."}]
giant_panda: [{'giant_panda': "A large, living mammal native to China, characterized by a distinctive black-and-white fur pattern with dark patches around the eyes, black ears, and black limbs, set against a white body. It has a robust, furry build, a rounded head, and a short snout. The animal is typically found in natural, outdoor environments such as snowy forests, and exhibits natural behaviors like eating or resting. Its physical features, including the texture of its fur and anatomical structure, are consistent with a biological organism, distinguishing it from artificial representations such as food items, costumes, plush toys, sculptures, paintings, printed images, or inflatable kites. The object must be three-dimensional, exhibit natural movement, and possess biological features such as fur texture and anatomical form to be classified as a true giant panda. Flat, two-dimensional depictions—such as printed images on cards, clothing, or surfaces, including printed images on functional objects like utensils or garments—despite resembling the animal, are excluded because they lack organic structure, biological texture, and life. Similarly, three-dimensional non-biological objects like inflatable kites or plush toys, even if they mimic the animal's appearance, are excluded because they lack biological texture, movement, and life. The presence of natural motion, biological texture, and a living, breathing form are key indicators of a true giant panda. Unlike other bear species such as brown bears, which have uniform brown fur and different facial features, the giant panda's unique coloration and anatomical traits are unmistakable."}]
grater: [{'grater': 'A non-edible, man-made kitchen utensil designed for shredding solid food ingredients, characterized by a three-dimensional, elongated body with a textured surface featuring fine, parallel, raised ridges arranged in a functional pattern. The object typically includes a handle or mounting mechanism for manual operation and is constructed from durable materials such as metal. It is used exclusively in food preparation to grate items like cheese, vegetables, or citrus zest, and must be a physical, functional tool capable of performing the action of grating. The grater is distinct from non-culinary objects, decorative items, toys, or tools with similar ridged surfaces but different functions (e.g., cleaning tools or heating appliances). It must not be a two-dimensional representation, illustration, or non-functional replica, and its defining features are the functional ridges and the structural design enabling food shredding.'}]
grizzly: [{'grizzly': 'A large, powerful terrestrial mammal with thick, coarse fur ranging from dark brown to light tan, a prominent muscular hump on the shoulders, a long snout, and a concave facial profile between the eyes and nose. It has small, rounded ears, a robust, bulky frame, and a distinctly massive build. The animal exhibits natural features such as visible claws, a realistic facial structure, and a wild, untamed appearance, with organic textures, realistic lighting, and natural movement. It is found in natural habitats such as forests, mountains, or tundra, and displays three-dimensional form, depth, and biological authenticity—distinguishing it from inanimate objects, toys, human figures, or artificial representations. The animal lacks human characteristics such as upright posture, facial features resembling a human, or clothing, and is not depicted in artificial or indoor settings such as hospitals or rooms with artificial lighting. It is not a man-made object, even if it has some organic-like features, and does not exhibit mechanical or artificial construction. The animal is a living, breathing creature with dynamic presence, natural behavior, and biological integrity, in contrast to static, non-living objects that may resemble organic forms but lack life, movement, and biological authenticity. It is not reflective, smooth, or polished, and does not have a metallic or manufactured appearance, ensuring it is clearly distinguished from inanimate objects such as utensils or tools. The animal must exhibit signs of life, including natural movement, breath, and biological processes, and cannot be a static, non-living representation or model. It must have a complete, three-dimensional, and biologically coherent form, including visible limbs, facial features, and a natural posture, which are absent in false positives. The animal does not display artificial adornments such as face paint, wigs, or costumes, nor does it exhibit human-like behavior or performance elements, ensuring it is clearly distinguished from human figures in disguise. The animal is characterized by a prominent muscular hump on the shoulders, a long snout, and a concave facial profile, which are more pronounced in adult grizzlies compared to younger bears or cubs. Juvenile bears, while sharing some features, lack the full development of these characteristics and are not classified as grizzlies.'}]
heater: [{'heater': 'A functional heating or ventilation device designed to generate and distribute heat or circulate air, typically through a heat-emitting element, fan, or air-moving mechanism. It is a working appliance with a protective grille, control interface (such as a digital display or buttons), and a mechanism for producing and emitting heat or moving air. The device is connected to a power source, often via visible wiring or a wall outlet, and is installed in an indoor or outdoor setting such as a living room, bathroom, office, or patio. It is not a decorative object, architectural feature, structural support, or non-functional utility fixture, and it must not be a human figure, clothing, or costume. It is distinguished from non-functional structures that may share a similar shape or vertical orientation, such as utility poles, towers, or decorative elements, by the presence of operational components and a clear purpose of environmental heating or air circulation. It is not a cooling appliance (e.g., refrigerator, air conditioner), a non-functional object, or a symbolic representation of a heater. It is typically a horizontal or vertical unit with vents or fins for air distribution, and its design and placement are consistent with a functional appliance rather than a static or non-operational structure. It may be portable and compact, but it must have visible operational features such as vents, control panels, or power connections, and it must not be a decorative dish, tableware, or non-functional item, even if it shares a similar size or shape. The object must not be a cleaning tool, such as a mop, or any other non-heating implement, regardless of its vertical orientation or size. It must not be part of a larger mechanical system (e.g., a vehicle) or have no heating function, even if it shares a similar shape or vertical orientation. It must not be a decorative item, such as a tiara, crown, or ornamental fixture, even if it has a similar form or vertical orientation, and it must not be a symbolic or representational object, such as a model or replica of a heater, unless it is a functional appliance. It is explicitly not a cooling appliance, such as a refrigerator or air conditioner, even if it shares a similar size, shape, or vertical orientation. It is also not a kitchen appliance, such as a toaster or microwave, even if it has a similar size, shape, or vertical orientation, and it must not be a non-heating device, such as a decorative lamp or a non-functional sculpture, even if it shares a similar form or vertical orientation.'}]
jeep: [{'jeep': 'A full-sized, functional, self-propelled, three-dimensional motorized land vehicle designed for transportation in rugged, off-road environments. It features a rugged, utilitarian design with a distinct front fascia including a grille, headlights, and bumper, integrated into a structured front section. The vehicle possesses a defined chassis and suspension system, allowing it to support a body and carry passengers or cargo. It has four wheels mounted on axles and connected to a drivetrain, with a complete mechanical system including a self-contained power source. The body is typically enclosed with a roof and side panels, though some variants may have an open-top design with a roll bar. The vehicle is typically painted in a military-style color, such as olive drab, and may feature insignia or markings indicative of its military or expeditionary use, including a star insignia on the side. The object must be a complete, functional vehicle, not a scaled-down model, toy, or partial representation of a vehicle. It must be a three-dimensional, physical entity, not a flat, two-dimensional image or representation, and must not be a non-transportation item such as a bag, container, accessory, or costume. It must be a self-propelled vehicle designed for movement on land, not a stationary structure such as a tower, monument, or building, and must not be a non-motorized object or a decorative representation of a vehicle. The object must not be a living organism, biological entity, or animal, and must not possess biological features such as limbs, hooves, or natural anatomy. The object must not be a non-transportation electronic device, appliance, or wall-mounted fixture, even if it has a mechanical appearance. It must not be a non-functional, scaled-down, or toy-like representation of a vehicle, nor a decorative item that merely resembles a vehicle part. Additionally, the vehicle must be motorized, with a self-contained power source and drivetrain, and must not be human-powered or reliant on external propulsion mechanisms. The front fascia, including the grille, headlights, and bumper, must be an integrated part of a complete, functional vehicle. The object must not be a two-dimensional graphical element, annotation, or digital overlay, and must be a tangible, physical entity with a complete mechanical system, not a representation or isolated component. It must not be a stationary object, such as a sculpture or utensil, that merely resembles a vehicle in form but lacks mechanical functionality, drivetrain, or the ability to move under its own power. The vehicle must have a spare tire mounted on the rear, a roll bar, and a distinct front fascia with a grille, headlights, and bumper. The overall structure is designed for stability and movement on various surfaces, powered by an internal combustion engine or an electric motor. This class of vehicle is distinguished by its mechanical complexity, transportation purpose, and specific design elements such as the front grille, headlights, and enclosed or open cabin, and is not a modern, closed-body SUV designed for urban or passenger use.'}]
kitten: [{'kitten': 'A young feline mammal characterized by a small, compact body, soft and fluffy fur, large round eyes, a rounded head, prominent ears, whiskers, and a tail; exhibiting natural biological features such as a feline facial structure, flexible limbs, and organic movement; possessing a non-mechanical, living form with typical feline anatomy and behavior, distinguishable from inanimate objects or artificial representations by its biological texture, dynamic motion, and inherent life processes.'}]
mat_(gym_equipment): [{'mat_(gym_equipment)': 'A flat, flexible, and soft surface designed for direct placement on the floor to provide cushioning, safety, or support during physical activity such as exercise, yoga, or movement. Typically made from pliable materials like foam, rubber, fabric, or natural woven fibers, the mat conforms to the floor and is used in gym, fitness, or recreational settings where structured physical activity occurs. It is not rigid, elevated, mounted, or used for non-physical purposes such as decoration, storage, structural support, cleaning, or food-related functions. The mat is not a floor covering used for aesthetic, cultural, or non-activity-related purposes, nor is it a piece of furniture, clothing, or protective covering. It is not associated with writing, personal hygiene, or small hardware items. The mat is generally of sufficient size to support a person or group during physical activity, may be rolled or folded for storage, and is distinct from cloths, towels, or other fabric items used for cleaning, drying, or decoration. It is not a food item, household appliance, container, or vehicle-mounted object, nor is it used for non-physical activities such as reading, resting, or display. The mat may be circular or rectangular and can feature patterns or designs, but its primary function remains physical activity support.'}]
pew_(church_bench): [{'pew_(church_bench)': 'A long, wooden bench with a backrest, constructed from multiple horizontal slats and supported by sturdy legs, designed for seating multiple individuals. It is typically found in a church or similar religious setting, positioned in rows along the length of a nave or aisle. The bench is functional and utilitarian, with a focus on durability and simplicity. Its identity is inseparable from its context, which is reinforced by the presence of religious symbols such as a cross, architectural features like columns or vaulted ceilings, and the arrangement of multiple identical benches in orderly rows, all indicating a place of worship. The bench must be situated within a space that exhibits clear indicators of religious function, such as liturgical furnishings, sacred art, or a formal, solemn atmosphere, distinguishing it from similar benches in secular or social environments.'}]
pinecone: [{'pinecone': 'A natural, three-dimensional, ovoid or conical seed-bearing structure from coniferous trees, characterized by a compact form composed of numerous overlapping, woody scales arranged in a spiral pattern around a central axis. The surface of these scales is typically rough, textured, and segmented, with natural variation in scale size, shape, and spacing, reflecting its organic origin. It must possess physical depth, a tactile, non-reflective surface, and a biological structure that is distinct from artificial or flat representations, including printed images, illustrations, or decorative patterns on surfaces such as paper, fabric, or architectural elements. The object must be a standalone biological entity, not a human figure, costume, or artificial imitation, and must not exhibit biological features such as limbs, facial features, or fur, which are characteristic of animals. Additionally, the object must have a segmented, scale-like texture and a spiral arrangement of scales, distinguishing it from smooth, uniform, or unsegmented natural objects, as well as from manufactured tools or devices with reflective surfaces, sharp edges, or linear mechanical components. The object must not be a man-made object, even if placed in a naturalistic context, and must not have a flat, two-dimensional appearance or a consistent, repeating geometric texture that lacks natural irregularity. The object must not be a digital image or display of a pinecone on an electronic screen, nor a decorative pattern resembling a pinecone on any surface, as such representations lack physical depth, tactile texture, and the natural, irregular spiral arrangement of scales. Furthermore, the object must not be a smooth, non-scale-like natural object such as a stone, seed pod, or nut, even if it is ovoid or conical, as it must exhibit the characteristic spiral arrangement of overlapping, woody scales to be classified as a pinecone. The object must not have a uniform, geometric, or machine-like appearance, even if it is ovoid or conical, and must display natural variation in scale size, shape, and spacing, reflecting its organic origin. It must not be a decorative motif, printed design, or stylized representation of a pinecone on any surface, including paper, fabric, or digital media, as such representations lack the physical and tactile properties of a true biological pinecone.'}]
poncho: [{'poncho': 'A wearable, three-dimensional textile garment designed to cover the torso and arms, featuring a central opening for the head and typically including a hood that extends down the back. It is constructed from flexible, water-resistant material and is form-fitting to allow ease of movement. The poncho is intended to be worn over other clothing for protection against rain or cold, particularly in outdoor environments. It is distinguished by its functional design for weather protection, full coverage of the torso and arms, and the presence of a head opening, which differentiates it from non-wearable objects, decorative items, or garments without such an opening. The poncho must be a human-made, tangible, and functional clothing item, actively worn by a person, conforming to the body as a protective garment, and not merely draped, displayed, or represented in a non-functional manner.'}]
postbox_(public): [{'postbox_(public)': 'A freestanding, vertically oriented, rectangular container mounted on a post at ground level in an outdoor public space. It features a narrow vertical slot on the front for depositing outgoing mail and is marked with an official postal service emblem. Constructed from durable, weather-resistant materials, it is designed for secure, long-term public access and is exclusively used for postal purposes. It is a fixed, non-portable structure with no other functional use, not affixed to a building or vehicle, and is not a model, replica, or decorative object. The presence of a mail slot and an official postal logo are essential defining features. It is a standardized, purpose-built public utility, not a generic storage, industrial, or personal container, and is not used for any purpose other than mail deposit.'}]
potholder: [{'potholder': 'A small, rectangular, non-living, man-made kitchen tool made of soft, pliable fabric with a quilted or woven texture, designed specifically for handling hot items or wiping surfaces. It is typically used for heat protection during direct contact with hot cookware or food, and must be actively used for its intended functional purpose in a kitchen environment. The object is distinguished by its compact size, flexible material, and practical use, and is not a container, utensil, decorative item, or full-body garment, even if placed in a kitchen environment. It is not a personal hygiene item, absorbent fabric product, or rigid, non-fabric object such as a canvas, board, stone block, or plastic item. It is also not a natural food item, such as a fruit or vegetable, even if it appears in a kitchen setting or is used in food preparation. The object must be actively used for its intended functional purpose in a kitchen context, and not merely present or worn as a decorative or novelty item. It is fundamentally distinct from living organisms, including animals, and is not a biological entity, regardless of its appearance or placement. It lacks biological features such as fur, eyes, or movement, and is not a natural or organic object. Additionally, it is not shaped like an animal or any other living creature, and does not have features such as limbs, a head, or facial features, even if made of similar soft fabric. It is not a wearable item, such as a raincoat or poncho, even if made of fabric, and is not used for personal protection from weather or environmental conditions. The object must be made of fabric, not rigid materials like metal, plastic, or stone, and must be used in a kitchen setting for its intended functional purpose, specifically for protecting hands from heat during handling of hot items, and not for cooking, storage, or serving. It must not be a decorative replica, model, or representation of a potholder, even if it resembles one in shape or size, and must be a functional, fabric-based tool used in a kitchen context. It must not be worn as a personal adornment, such as a tiara or headband, even if made of fabric, and must be used in a kitchen environment for its intended functional purpose. The object must be quilted or woven to provide insulation and must be used for heat protection, distinguishing it from other fabric items like napkins or placemats. It is a small, handheld item, not a large appliance or a rigid object, and must be actively used for its intended functional purpose in a kitchen context.'}]
robe: [{'robe': 'A loose-fitting, outer garment worn on the upper body, typically made of soft, flexible, and drapable fabric such as cotton, polyester, wool, or terrycloth, featuring wide sleeves, a draped silhouette, and a tied waist belt. It is designed to be worn by a person, allowing it to move with the body and conform naturally to the body’s shape. The garment must be a complete, functional piece of clothing, not a decorative or representational object (e.g., a cake decoration shaped like a garment, a crown, or a costume accessory such as a hat or wig) that mimics the appearance of a robe but lacks wearability and interaction with the human body. It must exhibit the characteristic drape and form of a wearable garment, distinguishing it from rigid, non-drapable, or non-functional items (e.g., costume pieces, armor, or non-drapable fabric items). The garment typically extends from the shoulders down to the lower body or feet, maintaining a full-length, flowing silhouette, and often includes a hooded or open collar. It is distinguished from flat fabric items (e.g., cloths, napkins, banners, or signs) and inanimate objects by its three-dimensional form, ability to drape, and functional purpose as wearable clothing. The fabric must be soft and flexible, enabling natural movement and conforming to the body’s shape, and the garment must be intended for practical use (e.g., lounging, bathing, or sleepwear) rather than ceremonial or decorative display. The garment must not be part of a rigid costume or armor ensemble, nor must it be made of hard, non-drapable materials such as metal or stiff plastic, even if it resembles a robe in shape. Additionally, the garment must be in a state that allows for wearability and interaction with the human body, distinguishing it from static, non-functional displays (e.g., hanging on a hook without being worn or used).'}]
saddlebag: [{'saddlebag': 'A saddlebag is a storage container designed to be functionally and structurally attached to the side of a motorcycle, typically positioned behind the seat and below the rear fender. It features a rectangular or elongated shape with a rigid or semi-rigid structure, constructed from durable materials such as leather or synthetic fabric. The container includes a main compartment secured by a flap fastened with straps, buckles, or a zipper, enabling secure transport of personal items during motion. It is actively serving as a transport accessory, with its attachment being integral and functional to its role, not decorative or incidental. The object must be a container and not a wearable item, clothing, or protective garment, even if positioned on a person or animal. It must not serve electronic, culinary, mechanical, or decorative purposes, and must be mounted via a structural system that enables its use in motion. The object must not be a cooking vessel, kitchenware, or household storage item, even if it shares a similar shape or is placed in a mobile context. The object must not be a structural or architectural feature, such as a tower or chimney, even if it is vertically oriented or located in a mobile environment. The object must be actively engaged in a mobile context, with its attachment and function directly supporting transport during movement. The container must be a functional storage unit, not a tool, appliance, or device, even if it is mounted or used in a mobile setting. Additionally, the object must be structurally attached to a mechanical vehicle (such as a motorcycle) and not to an animal, even if the animal is in motion. The object must not be a wheeled suitcase, rolling bag, or other travel bag that is placed on the ground or used independently in a mobile environment. The object must have a main compartment for storage and cannot be a non-container item such as a decorative patch, emblem, or label, even if it is mounted on a moving entity or shares a similar shape. The object must be mounted on a moving entity and actively serving as a transport accessory during motion, not a stationary object that merely resembles a saddlebag in shape or placement.'}]
saucepan: [{'saucepan': 'A cooking vessel typically featuring a round or oval base, deep sides, a single long handle, and often a lid, designed for stovetop use to simmer, boil, or sauté food.'}]
silo: [{'silo': 'Large, freestanding, vertical structures with a smooth, uniform cylindrical profile, typically used for storage in agricultural or rural landscapes. These structures are significantly taller than surrounding objects, stand in isolation or minimal integration within open natural environments, and exhibit no complex features, textures, or attachments. They are non-living, utilitarian, and not part of vehicles, personal equipment, or indoor settings. Silos are not portable, not associated with domestic or temporary uses, and are not found in cluttered, industrial, or urban contexts. Their presence is defined by dominance in the landscape, not by form alone, and they are not model or miniature representations, nor are they found in indoor or artificial environments such as model train setups, cake decorations, or kitchen settings. Silos are not part of any transportation system, including buses, trucks, or other vehicles, even if they have a cylindrical shape. They are also not small, flat, or food-related items that may resemble a cylinder in shape, such as tortillas or folded paper, and are not found in dining or domestic contexts. The defining characteristic is their large scale and prominent, isolated presence in open rural landscapes, distinguishing them from small, functional, or decorative cylindrical objects in non-agricultural settings.'}]
soupspoon: [{'soupspoon': 'A small, three-dimensional utensil made of a non-porous material such as metal, plastic, or ceramic, designed for scooping and consuming liquid foods like soup. It features a smooth, rounded handle and a concave bowl capable of holding liquid, with a compact, ergonomic shape optimized for direct use in eating. The soupspoon must be a functional, usable eating tool intended for food consumption, not a decorative object, serving container, electronic device, or symbolic representation. It must be a real utensil made of materials suitable for food contact, with a bowl capable of holding liquid, distinguishing it from non-utensil items such as costume props, oversized or non-functional objects, or items not intended for food consumption, even if they resemble a soupspoon in shape. Crucially, the object must be a three-dimensional, functional utensil and not a two-dimensional depiction, illustration, or representation of a spoon, even if it appears in a dining context. It must not be a serving dish, decorative container, or non-functional object, even if it has a spoon-like shape, as it is not intended for direct consumption or scooping of food. Additionally, it must not be a living organism, biological entity, or animal, regardless of visual resemblance or contextual placement. The soupspoon must be a tool designed for direct interaction with food during consumption, not a vessel for holding or presenting food, nor a structural or decorative item that may resemble a spoon in appearance or context. It must be a physical, three-dimensional object present in a dining context, not a reflection, image, or depiction of a spoon, even if it appears in a similar setting. The object must be visibly interacting with food, such as being partially submerged in a liquid food item, to confirm its role as a functional eating tool. It must not be a flat, two-dimensional object, even if it has a spoon-like shape, as it is not a utensil and cannot be used for food consumption. Furthermore, it must not be a serving dish, plate, or container, even if it is placed in a dining context, as it is not intended for direct use in eating. The soupspoon must be a standalone utensil, not part of a larger object or device, and must be used in a context where it is clearly intended for food consumption.'}]
mop: [{'mop': 'A cleaning tool consisting of a long handle attached to a flat, rectangular head made of absorbent material such as string, cloth, or synthetic fibers, specifically designed for mopping floors or cleaning large surfaces. It is typically used in a horizontal orientation on a surface and is associated with cleaning environments, distinguishing it from other objects with long handles or rigid structures that are not used for cleaning, such as decorative items, structural supports, ceremonial objects, or recreational equipment like baseball bats.'}]
telephone_booth: [{'telephone_booth': 'A freestanding, fully enclosed structure designed for private, human use to make telephone calls, typically located in public spaces such as sidewalks, train stations, or near buildings. It is constructed with durable, non-edible materials like glass, metal, or polycarbonate for the walls, supported by a frame, and topped with a roof. The booth houses a functional communication device, such as a payphone with a handset, keypad, and display screen, mounted on a central console. It features a door for entry and exit, providing a private space for making calls. The structure is stationary, not mounted on a vehicle, and is intended for public access. It must be a full-scale, structural, enclosed booth designed for human entry and private use, and not a non-functional representation, a food item, or a tableware item such as a cup or pitcher. Additionally, it must not be a natural object, animal, or any other entity that may superficially resemble an enclosure but lacks the intended function and construction of a telephone booth. The structure must be located in a public environment and not in a private indoor setting such as a bathroom or kitchen. It is distinct from non-enclosed signage, digital displays, or other public information devices, and excludes non-functional, decorative, or miniature replicas made of materials such as plastic, cake, fondant, or other artistic media, as well as scaled-down models or representations that are not operational or intended for human use. The object must be a real, functional, and operational structure, not a symbolic, artistic, or surreal depiction, and must not be a wearable item, costume, or prop, even if it resembles a booth in shape or materials. It must not be a non-enclosed structure, seating, or furniture, even if made of similar materials, and must be specifically designed for telephone communication, not for other purposes such as resting or storage. It must not be a handheld electronic device, such as a smartphone or personal communication device, nor any object that is portable, personal, or not designed for human entry, even if it has a screen or keypad. The structure must be a standalone, non-attached enclosure, not a functional accessory or storage item attached to a vehicle or other object, and must not be a non-enclosed structure, seating, or furniture, even if made of similar materials, and must be specifically designed for telephone communication, not for other purposes such as resting or storage. It must not be an enclosed structure designed for purposes other than telephone communication, such as ticket sales, information kiosks, or storage, even if it shares similar materials or design elements. The structure must not be an enclosed object used for cleaning, storage, or other non-communication purposes, even if it is located in a public space. It must not be a component of a vehicle, such as a window, door, or panel, nor a decorative item resembling a booth, even if it is made of similar materials or has a similar shape. The object must not be a food item, even if it is shaped like a booth or has a similar appearance, and must be constructed from non-edible materials intended for human use as a communication device, not for consumption. It must not be a household appliance, cleaning tool, or object used for domestic tasks, even if it has a similar shape or enclosure, and must not be used for any purpose other than telephone communication. The structure must be a fully enclosed, freestanding enclosure with a door, designed specifically for human entry and private use, and must not be a non-enclosed object such as a sign, digital display, or pole-mounted device, even if located in a public space.'}]
tiara: [{'tiara': 'A wearable decorative headpiece typically worn on the forehead, consisting of a slender band adorned with small, reflective, or jeweled elements arranged in a linear or symmetrical pattern. It may include additional embellishments such as flowers, motifs, or ornamental details, and is designed as a personal accessory for formal, ceremonial, or costume-related occasions. The tiara is distinct from non-wearable structural or functional items (e.g., signs, panels, equipment), decorative elements on consumable items (e.g., food), illuminated or functional lights, and temporary hair accessories used during styling (e.g., clips, pins). It also excludes large, rigid, or costume-specific crown-like structures that function as props or character-specific elements rather than wearable accessories.'}]
tiger: A large carnivorous feline with a distinctive coat of dark vertical stripes on orange-brown fur.
tortilla: [{'tortilla': 'A thin, round, flatbread made from corn or wheat flour, typically cooked on a hot surface such as a griddle or comal, with a soft texture and light golden color, often used as a base for various dishes in a meal setting; it is an edible food item, not a kitchen tool or utensil, and is distinct from non-edible inanimate objects such as appliances, containers, or bathroom fixtures, as well as from living organisms like animals or pets, and should not be confused with rolled non-edible items such as mats, towels, or fabric items, or with decorated food items like desserts or pastries that have toppings or icing; it is specifically a food item used in cooking or serving, not a structural or decorative object, and is commonly found in culinary contexts such as tacos, burritos, or as a side with sauces; it is always an edible, consumable food product, not a non-edible object, even if it shares a similar shape or appearance, and is made from dough or flour-based ingredients, not plastic, metal, or electronic components; it is typically uncooked or lightly cooked and is not a tool, implement, or household object, even if it is round or flat; it may be used as a base for toppings, but its identity is defined by its composition and preparation method, not by the presence of toppings; it is not a plush toy, fabric item, or any non-edible object, even if it resembles a tortilla in shape or color; it is specifically a food item made from dough or flour-based ingredients, cooked on a hot surface, and used as a base for various dishes, but must not be confused with other flat, round food items that are not made from traditional tortilla ingredients or preparation methods, nor with filled or folded dough products such as dumplings or pot stickers, nor with thick, baked flatbreads like pizza crusts or naan that are often topped, have a crusty texture, or are cooked in an oven; it is always a thin, pliable, and unadorned flatbread, not a food item with toppings, fillings, or a baked, crusty exterior.'}]
vacuum_cleaner: [{'vacuum_cleaner': 'A portable, motorized cleaning device designed for household or commercial use, characterized by a motorized suction system that actively draws in debris, a transparent dust collection chamber for storing collected particles, a handle for maneuvering, a nozzle for directing airflow, and a motorized head with a hose for cleaning carpets, floors, and other surfaces. It is typically powered by electricity and has a physical structure with mechanical components, distinguishing it from non-motorized or non-suction-based cleaning tools, decorative items, and printed or two-dimensional representations through its active cleaning function, mechanical components, and physical structure. The device is often cylindrical in shape, compact in size, and designed for easy maneuverability in various environments. It must be a real, three-dimensional object with functional mechanical parts, not a depiction, image, or representation of a vacuum cleaner.'}]
automatic_washer: [{'automatic_washer': 'A front-loading, rectangular household appliance with a circular door and a control panel on the top, featuring a smooth white exterior, typically used for washing clothes and often found in utility areas or laundry rooms, frequently stacked vertically with a dryer, and positioned near a sink or counter. The appliance is designed for actual use in laundry tasks and is not a model or decorative item, and is typically larger and more robust in construction compared to decorative replicas, and is distinct from industrial or commercial appliances in both size and design. It is a functional, operational device with mechanical components and is not a scaled-down or non-functional representation such as a cake decoration or toy. The appliance is a real, working device with internal mechanisms for washing and is not a static or non-functional object, nor is it a vacuum cleaner or other cleaning device with a similar cylindrical shape or mechanical components. It is specifically designed for domestic laundry use and is not a water cooler or similar utility device with a digital display and control buttons. The appliance is a real, working device with internal mechanisms for washing and is not a static or non-functional object, nor is it a person in costume or a telephone receiver, and is not a prop or decorative item resembling a washing machine.'}]
water_cooler: [{'water_cooler': 'A freestanding, upright appliance specifically designed for cooling and dispensing beverages, characterized by an integrated cooling mechanism, a reservoir, and a dispensing system (such as a spout or lever). It typically features a vertical rectangular or cylindrical body, a control panel, and may include branding or labeling. The appliance is engineered to maintain and dispense chilled or ambient beverages and is commonly found in public or shared environments such as offices, event spaces, or recreational areas. It is distinct from standalone beverage containers, portable coolers, storage boxes, non-functional decorative cabinets, or general-purpose refrigerators. While it may vary in design (e.g., freestanding, wall-mounted, or compact), it must include both cooling and dispensing components to qualify as a water cooler. It is not a general-purpose refrigerator, food storage unit, cooking implement, cleaning tool, or electronic device used for data transmission.'}]
wig: [{'wig': 'An artificial, wearable head covering made of synthetic or human hair, designed to mimic natural hair in texture, volume, and form. It is fully structured, three-dimensional, and intended to be worn on the head as a complete hair replacement, enhancement, or costume accessory. Wigs are distinguishable by their hair-like appearance, structural integrity, and function as a wearable, head-conforming hairpiece. They are not decorative attachments, non-hair-like display items, or objects placed on surfaces. Wigs are not hats, crowns, headbands, or fabric garments, nor are they food items, electronic devices, or inedible decorative objects that may resemble hair in shape. Wigs are not part of the wearer’s natural hair and are not functional devices (e.g., hair straighteners) or hair-like attachments on toys or dolls. Wigs are specifically intended to be worn on the head as a complete, functional hairpiece and are not static, non-wearable, hair-like structures on vehicles, buildings, or other surfaces, nor are they decorative elements on inanimate objects that resemble hair but are not intended for human wear. Wigs are artificial constructs and do not include natural hair or fur belonging to living organisms. They are not non-hair-like textiles, blankets, or garments draped over the head or shoulders, even if they have a hair-like appearance, as they are not designed to mimic natural hair or function as a complete head covering. Wigs are defined by their wearable, head-conforming, three-dimensional structure and intended use as a complete head covering, distinguishing them from flat, non-wearable objects that may resemble hair in shape or texture but are not intended for use on the head. Wigs are not tools or functional objects with hair-like components (e.g., brooms, brushes) even if they have a hair-like appearance, as they are not designed for human wear or to mimic natural hair on the head. Wigs are specifically intended to be worn on the head as a complete, functional hairpiece and are not decorative figurines, toys, or display items with hair-like components that are not wearable or head-conforming. Wigs are not small, non-conforming decorative items worn on the head (e.g., tiaras, headbands) that do not mimic natural hair in texture, volume, or form, nor are they non-hair-like objects (e.g., food, electronics) that may resemble hair in shape but are not intended for human wear. Wigs may be stylized, exaggerated, or colorful, and are often used in costumes or performances, but they remain distinct from non-wearable hair-like structures or non-hair-like objects.'}]
